sections.8.2: Yes. The AI chatbot will be hosted publicly on business.gov, and the interface will feature a clear, persistent disclaimer outlining the scope and goals of the tool, specifically clarifying that the output is informational and not a substitute for professional legal counsel. This aligns with transparency recommendations to publish clear information about the system's scope, capabilities, and limitations to set appropriate user expectations.
sections.8.4:
Yes.

- 8.4.1: Yes, users will be clearly informed that they are interacting with an AI system through a persistent disclaimer on the web-based chat interface on business.gov, which clarifies that the chatbot's outputs are informational and AI-generated.
- 8.4.2: Yes, a non-AI alternative is available. The project team has considered a static, comprehensive 'self-help' guide or checklist on business.gov as a non-AI alternative. To comply with accessibility and inclusion standards, this static guide should be made readily accessible to users who do not wish to interact with the AI chatbot.
sections.8.5: Yes. The chatbot will provide explanations by highlighting specific paragraphs in the uploaded cease and desist letters that appear to lack a legal basis and linking them directly to relevant business.gov guidance articles. This allows users to understand the specific factors and regulatory frameworks underpinning the AI's insights, aligning with the principle of providing clear, non-technical explanations that help users understand the logic behind AI-generated recommendations.

questions.why: The project outline lacks details regarding fairness definitions, stakeholder consultation, and team diversity, which are critical for completing these sections of the assessment.

items.text: Has the project team established a clear definition of fairness and selected specific qualitative or quantitative metrics to measure and monitor potential bias in the chatbot's legal analyses?
Has the project team conducted or planned any formal consultations with key stakeholders, such as small business owners, legal experts, or the business.gov legal team?
Does the project team include individuals with diverse skills, backgrounds, and experiences (e.g., legal, UX/UI, accessibility, and cultural diversity experts) to oversee the AI lifecycle?

gaps.reason: The project outline does not specify whether a clear definition of fairness has been established for the C&D Claim Evaluator use case.
The project outline does not specify any quantitative or qualitative measures for evaluating the fairness of the chatbot's outcomes.
The project outline does not indicate whether consultation has been conducted with the identified stakeholders (small business owners, business.gov legal team, etc.).
The project outline does not contain information regarding the diversity or multidisciplinary composition of the project team throughout the AI lifecycle.
